info.name: GitHub Installation API
Applications
GitHub List Installation Requests for the Authenticated App

params.name: per_page
params.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

params.name: page
params.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

items.docs: This API operation retrieves a paginated list of installation requests associated with the authenticated application. When an app uses the GET method on the /app/installation-requests endpoint, it receives details about organizations or users who have initiated but not yet completed the installation process for that app. The response typically includes information such as the requester's account details, request timestamps, and current status, allowing app developers to track pending installatio

info.name: GitHub List Installations for the Authenticated App

params.name: per_page
params.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

params.name: page
params.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

params.name: since
params.description: Only show results that were last updated after the given time. This is a timestamp in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

items.docs: The GitHub Installation API endpoint `/app/installations` with the GET method allows an authenticated GitHub App to retrieve a paginated list of all installations where the app has been installed. This operation requires the app to authenticate using a JWT (JSON Web Token) and returns details about each installation including the installation ID, account information, repository selection settings, permissions granted, and events subscribed to. This endpoint is commonly used by GitHub Apps to dis

info.name: GitHub Get an Installation for the Authenticated App

params.name: installation_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the installation.

items.docs:
Enables an authenticated GitHub App to find an installation's information using the installation id.

You must use a [JWT](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/apps/building-github-apps/authenticating-with-github-apps/#authenticating-as-a-github-app) to access this endpoint.

info.name: GitHub Delete an Installation for the Authenticated App

params.name: installation_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the installation.

items.docs:
Uninstalls a GitHub App on a user, organization, or business account. If you prefer to temporarily suspend an app's access to your account's resources, then we recommend the "[Suspend an app installation](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/apps/apps#suspend-an-app-installation)" endpoint.

You must use a [JWT](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/apps/building-github-apps/authenticating-with-github-apps/#authenticating-as-a-github-app) to access this endpoint.

info.name: GitHub Suspend an App Installation

params.name: installation_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the installation.

items.docs:
Suspends a GitHub App on a user, organization, or business account, which blocks the app from accessing the account's resources. When a GitHub App is suspended, the app's access to the GitHub Enterprise Server API or webhook events is blocked for that account.

You must use a [JWT](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/apps/building-github-apps/authenticating-with-github-apps/#authenticating-as-a-github-app) to access this endpoint.

info.name: GitHub Unsuspend an App Installation

params.name: installation_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the installation.

items.docs:
Removes a GitHub App installation suspension.

You must use a [JWT](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/apps/building-github-apps/authenticating-with-github-apps/#authenticating-as-a-github-app) to access this endpoint.

info.name: GitHub Get an Organization Installation for the Authenticated App

params.name: org
params.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

items.docs:
Enables an authenticated GitHub App to find the organization's installation information.

You must use a [JWT](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/apps/building-github-apps/authenticating-with-github-apps/#authenticating-as-a-github-app) to access this endpoint.

info.name: GitHub List App Installations for an Organization

params.name: org
params.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

params.name: per_page
params.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

params.name: page
params.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

items.docs:
Lists all GitHub Apps in an organization. The installation count includes
all GitHub Apps installed on repositories in the organization.

The authenticated user must be an organization owner to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the `admin:read` scope to use this endpoint.

info.name: GitHub Get Repository Installation for the Authenticated App

params.name: owner
params.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

params.name: repo
params.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

items.docs:
Enables an authenticated GitHub App to find the repository's installation information. The installation's account type will be either an organization or a user account, depending which account the repository belongs to.

You must use a [JWT](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/apps/building-github-apps/authenticating-with-github-apps/#authenticating-as-a-github-app) to access this endpoint.

info.name: GitHub Remove Repository from an App Installation

params.name: installation_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the installation.

params.name: repository_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the repository.

items.docs:
Remove a single repository from an installation. The authenticated user must have admin access to the repository.

You must use a personal access token (which you can create via the [command line](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/github/authenticating-to-github/creating-a-personal-access-token) or [Basic Authentication](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/overview/other-authentication-methods#basic-authentication)) to access this endpoint.

info.name: GitHub Get User Installation for the Authenticated App

params.name: username
params.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

items.docs:
Enables an authenticated GitHub App to find the user’s installation information.

You must use a [JWT](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/apps/building-github-apps/authenticating-with-github-apps/#authenticating-as-a-github-app) to access this endpoint.

info.name: Access
GitHub Create an Installation Access Token for an App

params.name: installation_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the installation.

items.docs:
Creates an installation access token that enables a GitHub App to make authenticated API requests for the app's installation on an organization or individual account. Installation tokens expire one hour from the time you create them. Using an expired token produces a status code of `401 - Unauthorized`, and requires creating a new installation token. By default the installation token has access to all repositories that the installation can access. 

Optionally, you can use the `repositories` or

info.name: GitHub Revoke an Installation Access Token

items.docs:
Revokes the installation token you're using to authenticate as an installation and access this endpoint.

Once an installation token is revoked, the token is invalidated and cannot be used. Other endpoints that require the revoked installation token must have a new installation token to work. You can create a new token using the "[Create an installation access token for an app](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/apps/apps#create-an-installation-access-token-for-an-app)" endpoint.

info.name: GitHub List App Installations Accessible to the User Access Token

params.name: per_page
params.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

params.name: page
params.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

items.docs:
Lists installations of your GitHub App that the authenticated user has explicit permission (`:read`, `:write`, or `:admin`) to access.

The authenticated user has explicit permission to access repositories they own, repositories where they are a collaborator, and repositories that they can access through an organization membership.

You can find the permissions for the installation under the `permissions` key.

info.name: GitHub List Repositories Accessible to the User Access Token

params.name: installation_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the installation.

params.name: per_page
params.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

params.name: page
params.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

items.docs:
List repositories that the authenticated user has explicit permission (`:read`, `:write`, or `:admin`) to access for an installation.

The authenticated user has explicit permission to access repositories they own, repositories where they are a collaborator, and repositories that they can access through an organization membership.

The access the user has to each repository is included in the hash under the `permissions` key.

info.name: Accessible
GitHub List Repositories Accessible to the App Installation

params.name: per_page
params.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

params.name: page
params.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

items.docs: The GitHub Installation API's GET /installation/repositories endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of repositories that the authenticated GitHub App installation has been granted access to. This operation returns metadata about each accessible repository, including repository names, IDs, permissions, and other relevant details. It's commonly used by GitHub Apps to discover which repositories they can interact with based on the installation's configuration and the permissions granted by the repo

info.name: Add
GitHub Add Repository to an App Installation

params.name: installation_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the installation.

params.name: repository_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the repository.

items.docs: Add a single repository to an installation. The authenticated user must have admin access to the repository.
